channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: politics

item.title: Trump grants one-month exemption for US automakers from new tariffs on imports from Mexico, Canada
item.description: President Donald Trump is granting a one-month exemption on his stiff new tariffs on imports from Mexico and Canada for U.S. automakers. Worries persist that the newly launched trade war could crush domestic manufacturing.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press, MPR News

item.title: Nevada Democrats react to Trump's speech; state Republicans applaud his vision
item.description: Democrats who represent Nevada in Washington, D.C., condemned what they see as a failure by President Donald Trump to lower prices for Nevada families, while state Republicans applauded his leadership the morning after his address to a joint session of Congress Tuesday night.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Federal judge blocks drastic funding cuts to medical research
item.description: A federal judge is preventing the Trump administration from drastically cutting medical research funding.
item.dc:creator: Lauran Neergaard And Michael Casey, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: San Diego congressmember pushes Trump for Nobel Peace Prize
item.description: A San Diego congressional representative is pressing the Norwegian committee who awards the prestigious Nobel Prizes to consider President Donald Trump to be the 2025 recipient of the peace prize.
item.dc:creator: Danielle Dawson, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Conservative justices 'stunned' by Supreme Court's USAID decision, lambaste majority in scathing dissent
item.description: Justice Samuel Alito penned a scathing dissent to the Supreme Court's decision ending a temporary pause on foreign aid payments through the U.S. Agency for International Development.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Dueling Al Green censure efforts stir frustrations within GOP as Republicans demand consequences
item.description: There are three different resolutions circulating to punish Rep. Al Green for repeatedly protesting President Donald Trump's joint address to Congress.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Snap polls reveal how speech watchers reacted to Trump's address to Congress
item.description: Most people who watched President Donald Trump's primetime address to a joint session of Congress had a positive opinion of what he spelled out in speech, according to snap polls.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump pleads with people of Greenland to join the US
item.description: (The Hill) - President Trump on Tuesday said he’d welcome the people of Greenland to join the U.S. as he stressed that “I think we’re going to get” the self-governed Arctic territory within Denmark. "I also have a message tonight for the incredible people of Greenland.
item.dc:creator: Julia Mueller, NewsNation

item.title: What Trump’s aid freeze means for Ukraine war
item.description: President Donald Trump’s freeze on U.S. aid going to Ukraine threatens to create severe problems for Kyiv in its war against Russia, leaving the country more vulnerable to aerial attacks on population centers and key infrastructure.
item.dc:creator: Ellen Mitchell, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Read NPR’s annotated fact check of President Trump’s address to Congress
item.description: President Donald Trump vowed to keep up his campaign of “swift and unrelenting action” in reorienting the nation’s economy, immigration and foreign policy as he delivered an unyielding address Tuesday night to Congress and the nation.
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press and NPR Staff, MPR News

item.title: Spanish-language rebuttal to Trump’s joint address focuses on immigration
item.description: New York Rep. Adriano Espaillat delivered the Democrats’ Spanish-language rebuttal to President Trump’s joint address, focusing on the president’s immigration policy.
item.dc:creator: Ximena Bustillo, MPR News

item.title: Trump's address to Congress showed the country's stark partisan divide
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — A president's speech to Congress — even without the formal gloss of a State of the Union address — is typically a time for a call to national unity and predictable claims about the country being strong. But that wasn’t President Donald Trump’s plan.
item.dc:creator: CHRIS MEGERIAN and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Trump praises major government cutbacks, efficiency measures
item.description: The president singled out specific spending programs, including an $8 million program promoting LGBTQI+ initiatives in Lesotho.
item.dc:creator: Damita Menezes, NewsNation

item.title: Swing state Sen. Elissa Slotkin to deliver rebuttal to Trump's speech to Congress
item.description: Michigan's first-term senator, Slotkin — a centrist with deep national security credentials — will deliver the Democrats' rebuttal to President Trump's first speech to a joint session of Congress.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea, MPR News

item.title: Nevada 'a perfect storm for homicides' in domestic violence cases, lawmakers told
item.description: FBI statistics show Nevada is "a perfect storm for homicides" and the state ranks third for men murdering women, the leader of SafeNest said Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Trump to pull funding from schools allowing 'illegal' protests
item.description: "All Federal Funding will STOP for any College, School, or University that allows illegal protests," Trump wrote on Truth Social on Tuesday.
item.dc:creator: Rich McHugh, NewsNation

item.title: 5 things to watch as Trump delivers joint address to Congress
item.description: President Trump will head to the Capitol on Tuesday for the first joint address of his second term emboldened by a month of mass federal agency slashing, shocking foreign policy moves and a dizzying array of executive actions that have disrupted Washington and beyond.
item.dc:creator: Alex Gangitano, NewsNation

item.title: DOGE cuts all GSA employees across Nevada, senator says
item.description: President Donald Trump and Elon Musk's Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE) fired all General Services Administration employees across Nevada on Monday, according to Democratic Nevada Sen. Catherine Cortez Masto.
item.dc:creator: David Charns, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Nevada film tax bill would bring jobs, but questions remain as lawmakers examine details
item.description: Lawmakers dug into details of the film tax credit bill on Thursday, with film studio executives from Warner Bros. Discovery and Sony answering questions in Carson City.
item.dc:creator: Greg Haas, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Elon Musk urges retired air traffic controllers to come back
item.description: Earlier this month, President Trump’s administration began firing hundreds of workers at the Federal Aviation Administration (FAA), including personnel brought on for the FAA radar as well as landing and navigational aid maintenance.
item.dc:creator: Filip Timotija, Fox 5

item.title: Lawmakers differ over location of Florida’s Black history museum
item.description: Florida has a rich and long history involving contributions from its Black residents. There are areas across the state highlighting those struggles and efforts. Some of those key moments are displayed at the Robert W Saunders, Sr Public Library in Tampa.
item.dc:creator: Ty Russell, WFLA, NBC
